introduction: This document examines the implementation of our new knowledge-sharing process introduced in March 2025. It focuses on the Knowledge Management team's efforts in driving adoption through workshops and training sessions. The analysis also provides insights into the impact of these workshops on teams interacting with the knowledge database. This report is crucial for understanding the effectiveness of our change management efforts.

subsections.heading: Overview of Knowledge Management Team Initiatives
subsections.content: The Knowledge Management Team has been consistently evolving since its inception back in 2018. By 2022, the team had implemented several critical initiatives to improve the overall knowledge sharing within Lee's Market. These initiatives ranged from streamlining communication channels, integrating more efficient knowledge management systems, to optimizing the database for easier access and navigation.
subsections.justification: This content discusses historical initiatives and doesn't provide information on the number of workshops conducted in March 2025.

subsections.heading: Insights into Workshop Attendance and Engagement
subsections.content: Lee's Market places a high value on employee engagement in workshops, regularly monitoring attendance rates and feedback. In 2023, an average of 87% of employees attended the scheduled workshops while the engagement rate, measured by active participation and post-workshop surveys, was approximately 76%. These metrics have been useful in planning future workshops and improving their effectiveness.
subsections.justification: The content provides historical data on workshop attendance and engagement but does not specify the number of workshops held in March 2025.

subsections.heading: Impact of Workshops on Knowledge Database Interaction
subsections.content: The workshops held by the Knowledge Management Team have been instrumental in enhancing the interaction with the knowledge database. For example, after the series of workshops in 2024, there was a 20% increase in the database usage rate and a 15% decrease in the number of reported issues. This clearly shows the positive impact of these workshops on the overall user experience.
subsections.justification: This content discusses the impact of workshops in 2024, and does not provide information about workshops in March 2025.

conclusion: The Knowledge Management team's efforts in driving adoption of the new knowledge-sharing process have yielded significant results. We've seen increased engagement and utilization of the knowledge database post-workshops. Moving forward, it is recommended to continue these workshops and consider more targeted sessions for teams requiring additional support. Continued monitoring of database interactions will enable us to further refine our training approach.
